category_title: Students with mild cerebral palsy developing self-evaluation and social bravery
minimal_context: Nancy is sitting in an individual interview during the first term to complete her 'Go for Goal!' self-evaluation report.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's begin our individual interview for the first term and fill out your 'Go for Goal!' self-evaluation report.

STUDENT: (Looks down at the desk) I don't want to do this self-evaluation right now.

TUTOR: Are there any difficulties that you might encounter?

STUDENT: I need to work hard, typing, rest... feeling nervous...

TUTOR: Just try your best to answer the questions on the report.

STUDENT: (Pushes the paper away and refuses to speak further) I can't do this interview. I don't know how to evaluate myself.

options.A: Implement an acknowledgment process using a ticket system as a tangible reinforcement.
options.B: Interview Nancy and other students in a group setting for the evaluation.
options.C: Use poems aimed at promoting reflection and reinforcement of mindfulness concepts.
options.D: Tell Nancy, 'Don't say you can't, you can,' to encourage her participation.
